headline: Berkshire Hathaway’s Energy Stocks Are Surging—but There’s a Catch
summary: Berkshire Hathaway’s  equity portfolio is getting a lift in 2026 from its two large energy holdings—  Chevron  and  Occidental Petroleum  But the company would have done better to have invested instead in a leading energy exchange-traded fund.  Occidental shares have risen 46% this year to $60.63, including a 3.9% gain on Thursday on a continued rise in  oil prices  which are up 3% to $110 a barrel in the session based on  Brent crude  Chevron has gained 33% this year to $201.81.  Berkshire began buying the Occidental stock in early 2022 and now holds 265 million shares, a 27% stake, that is worth $16 billion.

headline: Occidental Faces New Middle East Disruptions Testing Recent Share Price Strength
summary: Operational disruptions have hit Occidental Petroleum’s Shah gas field, linked to recent conflict in the Middle East. Crude loadings at Fujairah port are suspended, affecting Occidental Petroleum’s export and revenue channels. The disruptions introduce fresh operational risk for Occidental Petroleum, separate from day to day share price moves and analyst commentary. Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) now faces a direct hit to production and export activity from the Shah gas field and Fujairah...
